metadata.name: Skulk — Furtive Homunculus unblockable by higher-power creature
metadata.description: p1 controls Furtive Homunculus (2/1, Skulk). p2 controls Bladetusk Boar (3/2, Intimidate). p1 attacks with Furtive Homunculus. Under CR 702.118b, a creature with skulk can't be blocked by creatures with greater power. Bladetusk Boar has power 3, which is greater than Furtive Homunculus's power 2, so Bladetusk Boar cannot legally block Furtive Homunculus. p2 declares no blockers. Furtive Homunculus is an unblocked attacker and deals 2 combat damage to p2, reducing p2's life from 40 to 38. This script demonstrates the skulk evasion blocking restriction using the executable declare_attackers + declare_blockers harness pattern.
metadata.generation_notes: Furtive Homunculus oracle text (engine-verified from crates/engine/src/cards/defs/furtive_homunculus.rs): 'Skulk (This creature can't be blocked by creatures with greater power.)' P/T: 2/1. Mana cost: {1}{U}. Type: Creature — Homunculus. KeywordAbility::Skulk. CR 702.118b: 'A creature with skulk can't be blocked by creatures with greater power.' Bladetusk Boar (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/bladetusk_boar.rs): P/T 3/2, Creature — Boar, KeywordAbility::Intimidate. Power 3 > Furtive Homunculus power 2, so Bladetusk Boar CANNOT legally block Furtive Homunculus under CR 702.118b. Note: Intimidate on Bladetusk Boar restricts what can block IT, not what IT can block — it has no effect on Bladetusk Boar's ability to block, so there is no confound. p2 must declare no blockers. CR 510.1b: Unblocked Furtive Homunculus (power 2) assigns all 2 damage to p2. p2 life: 40 → 38. Furtive Homunculus (2/1) takes no damage (unblocked) — no SBA 704.5g. Bladetusk Boar takes no damage (was not declared as a blocker) — no SBA 704.5g. CR 704.5a: p2 has 38 life (above 0) — no player-death SBA. Script uses turn_number: 4 to ensure no summoning sickness on Furtive Homunculus (GameStateBuilder sets has_summoning_sickness: false for permanents in initial_state, consistent with scripts 106, 109, etc.). Initial phase is 'declare_attackers' per the established executable combat script pattern.

script.step_note: p1 declares Furtive Homunculus as an attacker targeting p2. Furtive Homunculus becomes tapped (CR 508.1f). Priority resets to p1 after attackers are declared.

actions.description: Initial: Furtive Homunculus (2/1, Skulk) untapped on p1 battlefield; Bladetusk Boar (3/2, Intimidate) untapped on p2 battlefield; life totals 40/40
actions.note: Furtive Homunculus is untapped and free of summoning sickness (turn_number: 4, builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false). Bladetusk Boar has power 3, which is greater than Furtive Homunculus power 2 — it cannot legally block Furtive Homunculus per CR 702.118b.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Furtive Homunculus as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1a: Furtive Homunculus is untapped and free of summoning sickness — legal attacker. CR 508.1f: Furtive Homunculus becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking. CR 508.1k: Furtive Homunculus becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Furtive Homunculus is tapped, stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Furtive Homunculus was tapped as part of the attack declaration. Priority is held by p1.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack — advance to DeclareBlockers step. p1 receives priority as active player in DeclareBlockers.

script.step_note: p2 cannot block Furtive Homunculus with Bladetusk Boar because Bladetusk Boar's power (3) is greater than Furtive Homunculus's power (2) — CR 702.118b. p2 declares no blockers. Furtive Homunculus becomes an unblocked attacker (CR 509.1h). Both players pass priority — advance to CombatDamage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares no blockers. CR 702.118b: Skulk — 'A creature with skulk can't be blocked by creatures with greater power.' Bladetusk Boar has power 3, which is greater than Furtive Homunculus's power 2 — Bladetusk Boar cannot legally be declared as a blocker for Furtive Homunculus. CR 509.1b: Any blocker declared in violation of a restriction is illegal; p2 correctly declares an empty blocker list. CR 509.1h: Furtive Homunculus has no blocking creatures declared — it becomes an unblocked attacker.

CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack — advance to CombatDamage step. The engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action on entering CombatDamage (no player command needed).

script.step_note: CombatDamage step: Furtive Homunculus (power 2, unblocked) assigns 2 combat damage to p2 (CR 510.1b). p2 life: 40 → 38. No SBAs apply.

actions.note: CR 704.5a: p2 has 38 life (above 0) — no player-death SBA. CR 704.5g: Furtive Homunculus (2/1) took no damage — no lethal-damage SBA. CR 704.5g: Bladetusk Boar (3/2) took no damage (was not a blocker) — no SBA. No SBAs apply.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after CombatDamage resolves. Stack is empty — advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: After combat damage: p2 at 38 life (lost 2 from unblocked Furtive Homunculus), p1 at 40 life, Furtive Homunculus still on p1 battlefield (tapped), Bladetusk Boar still on p2 battlefield (took no damage)
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Unblocked Furtive Homunculus (power 2) dealt 2 combat damage to p2. p2 life: 40 → 38. Furtive Homunculus remains on p1 battlefield (tapped from attacking, took no damage). Bladetusk Boar remains on p2 battlefield (was not declared as a blocker, took no damage). Skulk's blocking restriction (CR 702.118b) was enforced: Bladetusk Boar (power 3 > attacker power 2) could not block Furtive Homunculus.
